sections.paragraph:
δικάζω fut. δικάσω; 1 aor. 2 sg. ἐδίκασας La 3:58, mid. impv. 3 sg. δικασάσθω Judg 6:32; 1 fut. pass. δικασθήσομαι (Hom.+; Maximus Tyr. 3, 81 δ. θεός; pap; SEG XLIII, 850, 11, 986, 8 [both II b.c.]; LXX; TestAbr A 10 p. 87, 25 [Stone p. 22]; ApcEsdr [mid. ‘press a suit, go to law with’]; Ps.-Phocyl. 11; Philo; Jos., C. Ap. 2, 207; SibOr 4, 183; Tat. 6:1; Ath.) judge, condemn w. neg. (opp. δίκην τίνειν) Hm 2:5; Lk 6:37 v.l. (for κατα-, q.v.)—Schmidt, Syn. I 348–60. M-M. DELG s.v. δίκη.

fut. fut. = fut.

aor. aor. = aorist

  1. sg. = singular

impv. impv. = imperative

pass. pass. = passive (either of grammatical form or of passive experience); also used in reference to literary portion=passage

Hom Hom , VIII b.c.—List 5

Maximus Tyr Maximus Tyr , II a.d.—List 5

pap pap = papyrus, -yri

SEG SEG = Supplementum Epigraphicum Graecum—List 3

LXX LXX = Septuaginta, ed. ARahlfs, unless otherwise specified—Lists 2, beg.

TestAbr TestAbr = Testament of Abraham, with some interpolations; I b.c./I a.d.—Lists 2, 5

ApcEsdr ApcEsdr = Apocalypse of Esdras—List 2

Ps. Ps. = pseudo, pseudonymous

Philo Philo = P. of Alexandria, I b.c.–I a.d.—List 5

Jos. Jos. = Josephus. This abbr. used when follow by title; I a.d.—Lists 5

SibOr SibOr = Oracula Sibyllina, II–III a.d.—List 5

Tat Tat , II a.d.—List 5

Ath. Ath. = Athenagoras, II a.d.—List 5

neg. neg. = negative

opp. opp. = opposed to, opposite

Hm Hm = Mandates

v.l. v.l. = varia lectio (variant reading)

q.v. q.v. = quod vide (which see, see that which has just been mentioned)

Schmidt, Syn. Schmidt, Syn. = JS., Synonymik—List 6

M-M M-M = JMoulton/GMilligan, Vocabulary of Greek Testament—Lists 4, 6

DELG DELG = PChantraine, Dictionnaire étymologique de la langue grecque—List 6

s.v. s.v. = sub voce (under the word, look up the word)

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 246
